title: Arrays and indexed data

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Arrays and indexed data; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Arrays and indexed data
sources.claim: Arrays are ordered objects with a length and integer-indexed properties, supporting mutation and nonmutating transformations.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Arrays and indexed data
sources.claim: Arrays are ordered objects with a length and integer-indexed properties, supporting mutation and nonmutating transformations.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Arrays are ordered objects with a length and integer-indexed properties, supporting mutation and nonmutating transformations.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: slice returns a shallow copied range while splice mutates the source array. Your goal is to implement and test array traversal, update and copy operations. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Control flow and functions; pf data selection; Loops and iterable traversal. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Arrays are ordered objects with a length and integer-indexed properties, supporting mutation and nonmutating transformations. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Sparse holes, length assignment, reference identity, and in-place methods can surprise callers. This draft therefore rejects: slice and splice are interchangeable ways to copy an array section.

includes: Create, index, copy and mutate arrays with explicit bounds and sparse-array awareness.
Implement and test array traversal, update and copy operations.
excludes: Sparse holes, length assignment, reference identity, and in-place methods can surprise callers.

outcomes: Implement and test array traversal, update and copy operations.

examples.title: Arrays and indexed data: concrete case
examples.situation: slice returns a shallow copied range while splice mutates the source array.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: slice returns a shallow copied range while splice mutates the source array.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: slice is nonmutating, while splice mutates elements and length; choose from the ownership contract.
examples.result: The result follows because slice is nonmutating, while splice mutates elements and length; choose from the ownership contract.
